data.title: Choose Close CRM: Inside Sales Powerhouse
data.excerpt: See why 11,500+ teams pick Close CRM for faster sales with built-in calls, AI, and automation. Start your free trial now!

$media.alt: Choose Close CRM: Inside Sales Powerhouse

children.text: Are you tired of juggling multiple tools for your sales calls, emails, and follow-ups? Close CRM steps in as the all-in-one platform built specifically for inside sales teams, and it's changing how businesses sell. With over 11,500 companies trusting it and a stellar 4.7-star rating on G2, Close isn't just another CRM—it's a system designed to accelerate your sales from day one. If you're looking to streamline your operations and close deals faster, you'll want to see why Close is the top choice. Let me walk you through what makes it shine.
Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links; if you make a purchase through them, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.
Why Close CRM Stands Out for Inside Sales

Close was crafted from the ground up for teams that live and breathe sales. Unlike generic CRMs that require endless integrations, Close brings calling, email, SMS, and automated sequences under one roof. This means your reps can call leads directly from the CRM, send personalized texts, and set up follow-up sequences without switching tabs. The visual pipeline lets you track every deal in real-time, so nothing slips through the cracks. What's more, Close has handled over 2 billion calls, emails, and meetings, proving its reliability at scale. If you're serious about boosting your sales velocity, you should
explore Close CRM today
For a deeper dive into setup, check out this
step-by-step tutorial on using Close
Core Features That Make Sales Effortless

Let's break down the features that make Close a game-changer. First, integrated calling: you can make and receive calls directly within the CRM, with automatic logging so every interaction is captured. Second, SMS and email automation allows you to create multi-channel sequences that engage leads on their preferred platform. Third, the pipeline management is visual and intuitive, giving you a clear overview of your sales stages and helping you prioritize hot leads. Close also offers robust reporting to track team performance and identify bottlenecks. These tools work together seamlessly, reducing manual work and letting your team focus on selling. It's why businesses see quicker responses and higher conversion rates—because everything you need is right there.
Discover more about its applications in real-world scenarios with
Close use cases
Introducing Chloe: Your AI Sales Partner

One of Close's standout innovations is Chloe, an AI sales agent built directly into the platform. Chloe isn't a separate tool or clunky add-on; she's integrated into your workflow to handle initial lead engagement automatically. The moment a lead comes in, Chloe can call them, qualify prospects through natural conversations, and even book meetings—all while updating your CRM in real-time. This frees up your sales reps to focus on high-value conversations. Plus, with AI credits included in every plan (from 500 to 1,500 per user per month), you can leverage this technology without extra costs. Chloe exemplifies how Close is pushing boundaries in sales automation, making it a must-have for teams wanting to scale without adding headcount.
Stay updated on the latest enhancements by reading about
Close's new features
Who Should Use Close CRM?

Close is ideal for inside sales teams, startups, and small to medium businesses that rely on phone-centric sales and need a unified platform. If your team makes a high volume of calls, sends personalized emails, or uses SMS for follow-ups, Close is tailored for you. It's also perfect for businesses looking to automate repetitive tasks and leverage AI to qualify leads faster. Whether you're a solo operator with the Solo plan or a growing team on the Growth tier, Close scales with you, offering features that support rapid sales cycles. If you value efficiency, integration, and a tool that grows with your business, Close is designed to meet those needs.
For tips on maximizing its potential, explore
tips and best ways to use Close
Pricing That Scales With You

Close offers flexible pricing plans to fit different team sizes and budgets. The Solo plan starts at just $19 per user per month (or $9 annually), giving you core CRM features and 500 AI credits monthly. The Essentials plan at $49 per user per month (or $35 annually) includes more credits for small teams, while the Growth plan at $109 per user per month (or $99 annually) is geared toward leveraging advanced automation. All plans come with a free trial—no credit card required—so you can test drive Close risk-free. With annual savings up to 50%, it's a cost-effective way to invest in your sales infrastructure. Considering the time saved and deals closed, the value is clear.

With its intuitive design, powerful AI like Chloe, and all-in-one capabilities, Close CRM isn't just a tool—it's a partner in your sales success. Whether you're streamlining calls or automating follow-ups, it empowers your team to sell smarter and faster. Don't miss out on the opportunity to elevate your sales game;
give Close a try today

children.text: What is Chloe in Close CRM?
Chloe is an AI sales agent integrated into Close that automatically calls leads, qualifies them through conversations, and books meetings, updating your CRM in real-time to save your team time.
How much does Close CRM cost?
Close offers plans starting at $19 per user per month (Solo), with annual discounts up to 50%. All plans include AI credits and a free trial without requiring a credit card.
Is Close CRM suitable for small businesses?
Yes, Close is designed for inside sales teams of all sizes, from solo operators to growing businesses, with scalable features like integrated calling, SMS, and automation to boost efficiency.
Can I try Close CRM before purchasing?
Absolutely! Close provides a free trial for all plans, so you can explore its features, including calling, email automation, and Chloe, without any initial commitment.
